homepage.text_excerpt: David Lammy MP Skip to main content David Lammy MP From Tottenham, For Tottenham Join Get Involved Donate About Get in touch Help & advice Housing Immigration Benefits Advice & support organisations More Search Search the website Search Menu David Lammy Labour Welcome Thank you for visiting my website. I've spent the majority of my life representing the people of Tottenham and it remains a humbling privilege. If you or a loved one are in need of support, or you have a policy issue you would like to discuss, I encourage you to get in touch and I look forward to hearing from you. David Lammy MP Member of Parliament for Tottenham Prev Next Follow me on social media David Lammy MP Contact details Email: david.lammy.mp@parliament.uk Further details Promoted by Sian Eiles, on behalf of David Lammy, 28 Middle Lane, N8 8PL ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: About - David Lammy MP
pages.text_excerpt: About - David Lammy MP Skip to main content David Lammy MP From Tottenham, For Tottenham Join Get Involved Donate About Get in touch Help & advice Housing Immigration Benefits Advice & support organisations More Search Search the website Search Menu About From Tottenham, for Tottenham David Lammy is the Member of Parliament for Tottenham. His work is guided by his interest in justice, equality, and environmental issues. Born and raised in Tottenham, David studied law at the School of Oriental and African Studies (SOAS) Law School, was called to the Bar of England and Wales in 1995, and completed pupillage the following year at 3 Serjeants’ Inn. David completed an LLM (Master of Laws) at Harvard Law School in 1997 and worked at a US law firm as a barrister qualified in England and Wales and holder of a US postgraduate law degree. He returned to the UK to work at a British law firm, and upon entering Parliament in 2000 he registered as a non-practising barrister. Elected to represent the constituency of Tottenham, David replaced Bernie Grant, one of the first three black men elected to parliament. David rose quickly, holding positions in the previous Labour Government from 2001 to 2010. He is respected across the political spectrum for his strong, principled interventions on social justice issues, particularly in relation to race. These include his campaign to win justice for those affected by the Windrush Scandal and his prominent role in the fight for justice for the families affected by the Grenfell Fire. His 2017 review into the treatment of, and outcomes for, Black, Asian and Minority Ethnic (BAME) people in the Criminal Justice System remains an important point of reference. Between 2020 and 2024, David served in opposition as Shadow Justice Secretary and Shadow Foreign Secretary. After the General Election of 2024, the Prime Minister appointed him Foreign Secretary in government. As Foreign Secretary, David worked tirelessly to secure the United Kingdom’s formal recognition of a Palestinian state. In September 2025, David was appointed Deputy Prime Minister, Secretary of State for Justice and Lord Chancellor. At this time, he became the first black Deputy Prime Minister of the United Kingdom. David Lammy MP Contact details Email: david.lammy.mp@parliament.uk Further details Promoted by Sian Eiles, on behalf of David Lammy, 28 Middle Lane, N8 8PL ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: Get in touch - David Lammy MP
headings: h2: Contact me about a local issue
pages.text_excerpt: Get in touch - David Lammy MP Skip to main content David Lammy MP From Tottenham, For Tottenham Join Get Involved Donate About Get in touch Help & advice Housing Immigration Benefits Advice & support organisations More Search Search the website Search Menu Get in touch What can I help with?​ ​ Contacting Haringey Council, about schools, environmental concerns, anti-social behaviour, fly-tipping, parking or similar issues Connecting charities, organisations and businesses with each other who share comment interest Raising concerns about crime and anti-social behaviour with the police Assisting local businesses e.g. with business rates issues Raising issues with relevant Government Ministers or in Parliament (if other avenues have been exhausted) You can e-mail me any time at david.lammy.mp@parliament.uk, or please complete the form further down below. If you are a constituent seeking help, when you email please ensure you include: your full name your home address including postcode your contact telephone number any relevant reference numbers (such as a national insurance number or a complaint number) details about the issue you are facing, or policy issue you are concerned about My team and I aim to deal with most cases within a few weeks, however, urgent cases are given priority. Constituents who would rather talk to one of my caseworkers can call my constituency telephone: 020 7219 0767 Lines are open Tuesdays & Thursdays, 10am – 1pm. Lines are often busy. If you don’t get through immediately, please wait 10 minutes and call again. Due to the demand for assistance and the limited funding for staffing my office, I don’t have the capacity to review all requests and have to prioritise those in greatest need. If you are not a Tottenham resident you are unlikely to receive a response to your email. Due to strict parliamentary protocol, I am unable to take on cases for people who do not live in Tottenham. Contact me about a local issue Name First Last Email Telephone number Address and postcode Summary of your issue Please describe the issue and what you have already done to try to resolve it. Please include reference numbers if relevant If relevant, please attach supporting documents Max. file size: 36 KB. FAQs How long does it take to receive a response? I generally receive a response from Haringey Council and other organisations within two to three weeks I don’t live in the area but my relative does. Can you help? I need to gain consent directly from the individual concerned before I can make any enquiries on their behalf. If they are a Tottenham constituent, please ask them to get in touch with me and I will do my best to help them with their case. If they do not speak English well and would like you to act on their behalf, this is possible, as long as I gain consent directly from them. David Lammy MP Contact details Email: david.lammy.mp@parliament.uk Further details Promoted by Sian Eiles, on behalf of David Lammy, 28 Middle Lane, N8 8PL ©2026 Copyright Labour All rights reserved. pages.title: Help & advice - David Lammy MP
pages.text_excerpt: Help & advice - David Lammy MP Skip to main content David Lammy MP From Tottenham, For Tottenham Join Get Involved Donate About Get in touch Help & advice Housing Immigration Benefits Advice & support organisations More Search Search the website Search Menu Help & advice How I can help Part of my role as an MP is to try to help constituents who are facing difficulties. I can help with a range of issues, including immigration, housing, and benefits. Please click on the links below to find out more about how I can help with these issues. Please note that due to strict parliamentary protocol, I can only help people who live in the Tottenham constituency. If you are unsure who your local MP is, you can check by entering your postcode here .
